items.q: Why does deforestation increase flood risk?
items.correct: Trees intercept rainfall, their roots increase infiltration and transpiration removes water — removing them increases surface runoff and reduces lag time, peaking river levels faster.
items.misconception: Deforestation causes floods because trees normally hold water in their branches forever.
items.random_wrong: Deforestation has no effect on rivers — only rainfall matters.
items.why: Interception is temporary but it delays and reduces runoff. Without forests, water reaches rivers faster and in greater volume — interacting with antecedent soil moisture to drive flood peaks.

items.q: Why do informal settlements develop in rapidly growing LIC cities?
items.correct: Rural-urban migration outpaces formal housing supply; migrants build their own homes on marginal land without secure land tenure — driven by housing shortage, not by personal choice.
items.misconception: People live in slums because they prefer them or are too lazy to find better housing.
items.random_wrong: Informal settlements are built by the government for new migrants.
items.why: Self-construction is a response to formal-sector failure. Many residents are employed and pay informal rent. Students moralise; the geographical analysis is about supply, tenure and land economics.

items.q: How do scientists know current climate change is anthropogenic?
items.correct: Isotopic signatures of CO2 match fossil fuel sources, warming patterns (cooler stratosphere, warmer troposphere) match greenhouse forcing, and natural forcings cannot explain the rate.
items.misconception: Climate change is mainly caused by natural Milankovitch cycles.
items.random_wrong: Climate change is caused by the sun getting hotter.
items.why: Solar output has been flat or slightly declining since 1980 while temperatures rose sharply. Natural cycles operate on tens of thousands of years; the current change is too fast.

items.q: Who designed the Human Development Index in 1990?
items.why: Correct answer: Sen and Haq. “Stiglitz and Sen” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many Sustainable Development Goals were adopted in 2015?
items.why: Correct answer: 17. “15” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did the SDGs replace the MDGs?
items.why: The real figure is 2015 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
